LayoutData.CloseMessage: Do you want to close this popup?

Children.Text:
This dialog will help you create an installer payload that can be used to import certain files and settings of this project to another project.

> This is mainly useful if you intend to publish a product on the HISE store.

This dialog will help you create a `install_package.json` file that contains instructions on what files to install from this repository when the asset is installed.

___

Children.Help:
Untick this box if you don't want to load any existing settings.

This will check whether a `install_package.json` file exists in the repository and then load its content so you can modify an existing install script.

Children.Text:
Here you can add all actions & file filters that should be performed when the install script is performed.

> Note that by default the install script would install every single file from the project so you probably might want to reduce the payload using the file type filters & wildcards!

Children.Help:
If this is not empty, the asset manager will show this message whenever a user installs the asset so you can add important installation instructions.

> This supports markdown format.

Specify wildcards (one per line) that will be used to filter all files.

> If a wildcard contains a `*` character, then it will use true file pattern matching, otherwise it will just filter from the relative path of the project (so eg. `Scripts/` will include all script files.

If this is empty, it will use the default catch-all wildcard `*`. Multiple wildcards will be combined so that every file that matches any of the wildcards is included in the payload.

Use this wildcard to exclude files from being included in the payload.

These wildcards will be used to filter out all results that match the given wildcard, so eg. `*.txt` will prevent any file with the file extension `.txt` from being included in the payload.

> Note that it will automatically exclude all build files, hidden files, special HISE files (eg. PooledResources). so you just need to add file filters that exclude files specific to your project.
If enabled, then this will prefilter the files to only allow the specified directories.

Children.Items:
Scripts
AdditionalSourceCode
Samples
Images
AudioFiles
SampleMaps
MidiFiles
DspNetworks

Children.Help:
Enable this and select all preprocessors from the current `project_info.xml` setting file to be included in the installer payload.

> This will add / overwrite the preprocessors of the HISE project that you install this asset to.
If enabled, the install process will copy a given string to the system clipboard (eg. script code that includes the scripts) or a HISE snippet) to help the user with the integration
Enter the text string that you want to copy to the clipboard after the installation process

Children.Text:
Press Finish in order to write the install script to the `install_package.json` file. You can also simulate a test run that prints all actions to the console that would be perfomed if this script was to be used.

> If you supply a .zip file from another project, the simulator will use the content of this zip file instead of the current repository's files so you can test how a installation of another project would work.

___

Children.Help:
Select any zip file to be used as source for the simulation test run.

> This must be a zipped HISE project folder that contains both the `project_info.xml` file as well as the `install_package.json` install script in the root folder.

Children.Tooltip: Click to simulate the install payload
